findings.summary: USB D+/D- differential pairs on OTG connectors not detected; ESD protection ICs (ESDAxxSC6) correctly detected on both OTG USB buses; PMEG2010EJ Schottky diodes (D1-D3) misclassified as reverse_pol...

correct.description: U3 protects OTGFS_DN/OTGFS_DP and U4 protects OTGHS_DN/OTGHS_DP, both detected as esd_ic type protection devices with correct net listings.

incorrect.description: D1, D2, D3 are PMEG2010EJ Schottky diodes used as power path ORing diodes (common anode on VBUS inputs, cathode to +5V0 bus), not reverse polarity protection. They are forward-biased power-path selectors, not reverse-polarity clamps. The protected_net and clamp_net are both '+5V0' which is wrong for a protection device.

missed.description: X1 and X2 are USB-OTG connectors with D+/D- signals routed through 22Ω series resistors to the STM32 USB FS and HS peripherals. differential_pairs[] is empty. The design also has ESD protection (U3/U4 ESDAxxSC6) on both USB buses. The USB buses should be detectable as differential signal paths.
